source_text: ‚ÄĘPer-variable Big-M bounds.For model type z, GPU type g, tensor-paralel degree t, and batch size b, the standard indicator constraint linking nz,g,t,band yz,g,t,bis:nz,g,t,b‚Č§M ¬∑yz,g,t,b, where Mis a sufficiently large constant; a naive formulation uses a single global bound M= maxg‚ąąG őļg, which is overly loose for most variables.

source_text: The indicator constraint becomes: nz,g,t,b‚Č§Mz,g,t,b ¬∑ yz,g,t,b (20) t  bounds the number of replicas by available GPU capacity,  őĽz b  bounds the number of replicas required to cover the workload.The term per-variable emphasizes that each decision variable nz,g,t,b has its own tight upper bound, instead of sharing a single global constant.This significantly tightens the LP relaxation:when yz,g,t,btakes fractional values, the feasible range of nz,g,t,bis correspondingly restricted.As a result, the solver obtains stronger bounds and can prune the branch-and-bound search tree more effectively.

source_text: minTbalanced, s.t.Tbalanced‚Č•Lz, ‚ąÄz‚ąąM (21) Lz‚Č•őõz,g,t,b ¬∑ yz,g,t,b, ‚ąÄg‚ąąG,t ‚ąąTg,b ‚ąąBz (22) Lzcaptures the completion latency of model Mz, determined by its slowest active replica group.While minimizing Tbalancedensures fairness across models, multiple solutions may achieve the same optimal makespan but differ in individual model latencies.To guide the solver toward better practical solutions, the LLM introduces a weighted objective (zindexes models in increasing order of size, i.e., a larger z corresponds to a larger LLM):

translation: This strategy assigns larger weights to larger LLMs, encouraging the solver to reduce their latency when possible.Compared to an unweighted and very small secondary term, this stronger formulation provides a clearer opti
info_points: This strategy assigns larger weights to larger LLMs, encouraging the solver to reduce their latency when possible.Compared to an unweighted and very small secondary term, this stronger formulation provides a clearer optimization signal, helping the solver distinguish between solutions with identical
source_text: This strategy assigns larger weights to larger LLMs, encouraging the solver to reduce their latency when possible.Compared to an unweighted and very small secondary term, this stronger formulation provides a clearer optimization signal, helping the solver distinguish between solutions with identical makespan and converge faster to high-quality solutions.

translation: Optimization on ILP solver.The ILP solver, i.e., CBC solver, was carefully tuned by the LLM. We summarize the details in Table 7.These settings directly accelerate ILP convergence while maintaining solution quality withi
info_points: We summarize the details in Table 7.These settings directly accelerate ILP convergence while maintaining solution quality within a negligible 0.3% optimality gap.
source_text: Optimization on ILP solver.The ILP solver, i.e., CBC solver, was carefully tuned by the LLM. We summarize the details in Table 7.These settings directly accelerate ILP convergence while maintaining solution quality within a negligible 0.3% optimality gap.
